summary: REQ-2 appears implemented in MetricsPanel with explicit normalization to show “No objects detected” and improved visual hierarchy. REQ-1 status-color coding changes for DriverAlertPanel are not evidenced in the provided diff snippet (only audio/throttling and animation are visible). REQ-3 is claimed in the file summary for roadDetection.ts (determinism + non-square morphology fixes), but the truncated code excerpt does not show the specific segmentation/morphology changes; still, there is some evidence via the provided frontend-file-summaries description.

missing_requirements.requirement: Update the Driver Alerts UI to use clear status color-coding: warning/danger/critical in red, safe/normal in green; apply consistently across DriverAlertPanel elements and remain readable in light/dark themes.
missing_requirements.reason: The diff excerpt for DriverAlertPanel shows logic changes (audio alerts, throttling, pulse glow) but does not show any concrete className/style changes that apply green styling for the no-alert state and red styling for any alert state. With the content truncated, there isn’t sufficient evidence that the required red/green status color-coding was implemented consistently across headings/labels/badges/borders.

hallucinated_features.description: Modified an immutable hook file (useInternetIdentity.ts), despite the constraint to not edit it.
hallucinated_features.reason: BuildRequest constraints explicitly state not to edit frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, but it appears in the modified files list.

hallucinated_features.description: Added a new React Query hook for system-wide detection statistics with periodic refetching (useGetDetectionStatistics).
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested by any requirement (REQ-1/2/3) and not part of stated goals.

hallucinated_features.description: Enhanced global styling/theme system (new cyan/teal OKLCH palette, neumorphic utilities, glow effects) and Tailwind config extensions.
hallucinated_features.reason: Not requested by the BuildRequest requirements; could also conflict with REQ-1’s requirement to use red/green primary status colors for alerts.
